content:
A good project for the future!
unfortunately, for now it’s more useless than useful (even if temporarily)
If I have the strength and time, I might help with developing the functionality (thanks to the open github page)

What is that you would expand? 
We are open for suggestion and contributions, and a way to inport and export from the game is in the works.

If the site positions itself as a database + team building, then it's not doing very well yet
There's a lack of information about characters, except for having their picture and name. In team building, there’s no option to choose copies, which also matters (and sometimes very much), not to mention covenant sets and their builds (which I would also like to see added)  
I understand that this isn’t something that can be done quickly, but for now, the site seems useless (harmless), and it’s faster to just go into the game

The database portion is not implement at the moment, that is a work in progress that will be added later.
The major utility of the teambuilder is to more easily organize the teams for dtide, primarily, so additional copies are deliberately not included (we could make a toggle for it).
We already have covenants sets in the selection, inserting their individual pieces seems redundant considering you can reuse them for multiple teams

Is true that some part can and will be expanded to make it even more effective to use, but they will be implemented in time. 
If you have suggestions for effective qol or others, free to share them.

Hmm, I thought the team builder was more designed to conveniently share a team for a particular situation (say, D-Tide), in which case it would be possible to indicate copies of wheels and characters, and the level of upgrade (minimum), to minimize the information that needs to be added for someone to understand the potential investments 
But if we consider it for personal use and optimization, overall, all of the above is indeed unnecessary
I think it already depends on the potential ambitions of the application itself. But I shared my initial thoughts on this, although they could be made more concrete with a clear idea
In D-tide you can't use the same unit or wheels in more than one team, so the restriction is necessary for it
Ah, if you mean the dupes levels, it is in the work right now

Overall, I would say that it's probably unnecessary to add a dependency on D-tide waves in any way. So here, it's more about just considering a pure team builder. And I think the players themselves will figure out how to allocate their investments as they need.  

At most, you could add the ability to set a wave in D-tide for the team (a temporary team for a specific tide, that can simply be separated by dates), or a mode that the team is intended for. But again, this probably depends more on the resources in the project

I mean, considering that you can export and import teams with the codes, one can still organize their teams in case of duplicates that way.
At the moment the major use of the team builder is to organize your unit and wheels distribution for d-tide a bit better (more functionality for it will be released soon).
The idea of porting the team to a wave management screen is interesting, will see if it is a possibility.
Still, if you want to discuss more in depth about the scope and possibilities, i suggest to contact the developer directly from the contact present in there

yep 
I'll think about something like that, I might even work on some things myself directly in the project
Yeah, i honestly thing you should discuss it with the developer at this point, can i share your contact?
I'll write it myself, no need, but thanks
As in the code? ok, even if a direct collaborative effort is always appreciated
I'm not very good at coding, but I have some knowledge
Okky, free to approach it as you like

- Database functionality is not implemented whatsoever yet, the /characters page is just placeholder and should probably not even exist to be honest.. So the website is indeed lacking in that department. The goal, once I am happy with the builder and all major features are in place, is to start on the actual database UI which will contain a lot more in depth information about the characters, wheels, and other features (TBD)
- Ownership tracking and display for dupes/collected state is is coming soon, hopefully later today, with local storage caching so you don't lose your builds and collection when leaving the page -
With an optional "save as file" feature since we do not have any accounts and back-end to store things on.
- Covenants are intentionally left "dumb", I understand there are cases where certain characters prefer a 50/50 covenant set or other odd configuration, but it's not likely something we will support any time soon, and making your own "sets" like Crit/Kflare stats is kinda out of scope honestly, especially since the website export/import has no support for anything like it.

Regardless we appreciate the feedback you have brought <:pray:1356053116260454534>
